articles.title: Costco Wholesale (NASDAQ:COST) Releases Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.07 EPS
articles.summary: Costco Wholesale (NASDAQ:COST) reported Q2 earnings of $4.58 per share, exceeding analyst estimates by $0.07, and revenue of $69.60 billion, beating expectations. Despite strong performance including 7.4% comparable sales growth and a 13.6% increase in membership fee income, the stock saw a dip of $24 after the report due to mixed margin dynamics, tariff uncertainties, and some insider selling. Analysts, however, hold a generally "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average price target of $1,016.18, and the company plans to open approximately 28 new warehouses in FY26.

articles.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Target (NYSE:TGT) Stock
articles.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has increased its price target for Target (NYSE:TGT) stock to $120.00 from $115.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, despite potential downside from the previous close. This adjustment follows Target's strong Q4 earnings beat and a multi-year turnaround plan that includes significant investments and new store openings. While analysts acknowledge bullish catalysts, the consensus remains a "Hold" due to lingering revenue weakness and execution risks associated with the turnaround strategy.

articles.title: Target opens 30 stores this year, including 'food-forward' prototype
articles.summary: Target plans to open 30 new stores this year, reaching a total of 2,000 locations. This expansion is part of a $5 billion investment aimed at reversing recent quarterly sales declines. One of the new store formats will include a "food-forward" prototype.
